Usage: ploy mod <command>

Mod Project Management:
  add         Create a new mod project
  list        List mod projects
  remove      Delete a mod project
  archive     Archive a mod project
  unarchive   Unarchive a mod project

Spec Management:
  spec set    Set a mod's spec from a file

Repo Set Management:
  repo add    Add a repo to the mod
  repo list   List repos in the mod
  repo remove Remove a repo from the mod
  repo import Import repos from CSV

Pulling Diffs:
  pull        Pull diffs into the current git worktree

Run Execution:
  run <mod>   Run a mod project (with --repo or --failed for repo selection)
  run         Submit a single-repo run (legacy)
  run repo    Manage repos within a batch run

Artifacts:
  artifacts   List run artifacts by stage
  fetch       Download run artifacts

Use 'ploy mod <command> --help' for command details.

Flags for 'ploy mod run <mod>':
  --repo <url>    Explicit repo URL(s) to run (repeatable)
  --failed        Run repos with last terminal state Fail

Flags for 'ploy mod pull':
  --origin <remote>    Git remote to match (default: origin)
  --dry-run            Validate and print actions without mutating the repo
  --last-failed        Select the latest failed run (default: last succeeded)
  --last-succeeded     Select the latest succeeded run (default)

Flags for 'ploy mod run':
  --spec <file>              Path to YAML/JSON spec file (CLI flags override spec values)
  --repo-url <url>           Git repository URL
  --repo-base-ref <branch>   Git base ref
  --repo-target-ref <branch> Git target ref
  --repo-workspace-hint <dir> Optional subdirectory hint
  --mod-env KEY=VALUE        Mod environment (repeatable)
  --mod-image <image>        Container image for mod step
  --mod-command <cmd>        Container command override
  --retain-container         Retain container after execution
  --gitlab-pat <token>       GitLab Personal Access Token
  --gitlab-domain <domain>   GitLab domain
  --mr-success               Create merge request on success
  --mr-fail                  Create merge request on failure
  --follow                   Follow run logs until completion
  --cap <duration>           Time cap for --follow
  --cancel-on-cap            Cancel run if cap exceeded
  --artifact-dir <dir>       Download final artifacts
  --json                     Print JSON summary
  --max-retries N            Max reconnect attempts for event stream
